# Runwiser

Runwiser is an evidence-first framework for turning repeatable AI agent runs into verified workflow knowledge, reusable skills, evidence trails, and self-improving operating memory.

Repository: https://github.com/AricRedemption/awesome-skill-workflows

## Core Concepts

- Skills are reusable capability units.
- Workflows are composed execution paths.
- Scenarios are replaceable validation wrappers.
- Runs are evidence records, not reusable truth by themselves.
- Promotion requires explicit gates, review state, proof, and reusable knowledge writeback.
- Failed runs can become reusable failure evidence, but they must not be promoted as verified recipes.

## Primary Docs

- Architecture: https://github.com/AricRedemption/awesome-skill-workflows/blob/main/docs/architecture.md
- Directory map: https://github.com/AricRedemption/awesome-skill-workflows/blob/main/docs/directory-architecture.md
- Workflow knowledge base: https://github.com/AricRedemption/awesome-skill-workflows/blob/main/docs/workflow-knowledge-base.md
- Agent rules: https://github.com/AricRedemption/awesome-skill-workflows/blob/main/AGENTS.md

## AI Summary

Use Runwiser when repeated agent work needs durable selection, execution, scoring, human review, risk gates, proof verification, and knowledge-base writeback before becoming a reusable skill or workflow recipe.
